expectations.evidence: All feature IDs follow F-X.Y.Z format: F-3.6.65 through F-3.6.74
expectations.text: correct_feature_ids — All feature IDs match F-X.Y.Z format

expectations.evidence: All requirement IDs follow R-X.Y.Z format: R-3.6.65 through R-3.6.74. No letter-suffixed NFRs present
expectations.text: correct_requirement_ids — All requirement IDs match R-X.Y.Z or R-X.Y.Za/b/c format

expectations.evidence: User story IDs use US-X.Y.Za/b/c format (letter-suffixed) instead of pure US-X.Y.Z numeric: US-3.6.65a, US-3.6.65b, US-3.6.65c, US-3.6.66a, etc. The letter suffix is not a numeric Z component
expectations.text: correct_story_ids — All user story IDs match US-X.Y.Z format

expectations.evidence: All IDs use domain 3.6 (Procedural Generation), appropriate for biome-driven terrain
expectations.text: correct_domain — IDs use an appropriate module number for the topic

expectations.evidence: Each feature has exactly 1 requirement: F-3.6.65 has R-3.6.65, F-3.6.66 has R-3.6.66, F-3.6.67 has R-3.6.67, etc. No feature has 2+ requirements
expectations.text: one_to_many_requirements — At least some features have 2+ requirements

expectations.evidence: No non-functional requirements with letter suffixes exist. All requirements are functional only (R-3.6.65 through R-3.6.74) with no NFR variants
expectations.text: nfr_suffixes — Non-functional requirements use letter suffixes (a, b, c)

expectations.evidence: Multiple features have 2+ stories: F-3.6.65 has 3 stories (US-3.6.65a level designer, US-3.6.65b level designer, US-3.6.65c level designer); F-3.6.73 has 4 stories from player and level designer personas
expectations.text: one_to_many_stories — At least some features have 2+ user stories from different personas

expectations.evidence: 6 distinct personas: level designer, environment artist, technical artist, player, creative director — plus implicit persona variations. Explicit: level designer, environment artist, technical artist, player, creative director
expectations.text: varied_personas — User stories reference at least 3 distinct personas

expectations.evidence: All 10 requirements use SHALL: e.g., R-3.6.65 'The engine SHALL provide an editor brush tool', R-3.6.68 'The engine SHALL generate terrain heightmap detail per tile'
expectations.text: shall_in_requirements — Requirements use SHALL or SHOULD language

expectations.evidence: features.md contains no instances of SHALL. Uses descriptive language: 'An editor brush tool that lets designers paint', 'A biome definition asset that bundles', 'Smooth blending between adjacent biome regions'
expectations.text: no_shall_in_features — Feature file does not contain SHALL

expectations.evidence: All 10 requirements include Rationale and Verification subsections with specific test procedures
expectations.text: rationale_and_verification — Requirements include Rationale and Verification sections

expectations.evidence: All user stories include Acceptance criteria, e.g., US-3.6.65a: 'I can select a biome type and paint it onto the world map with immediate visual feedback'
expectations.text: acceptance_criteria — User stories include Acceptance criteria

expectations.evidence: Features extend beyond core terrain: F-3.6.73 (Biome Weather Zone System) and F-3.6.74 (Biome Atmospheric and Lighting Profiles) go beyond the terrain generation core idea into weather and atmosphere
expectations.text: feature_expansions — Output includes features beyond the literal idea (suggested additions)

expectations.evidence: Cross-domain deps: F-3.6.66 depends on F-1.1.1 (ECS), F-3.6.73 depends on F-11.4.1 (Rain), F-11.4.3 (Snow), F-11.4.4 (Fog), F-11.4.5 (Lightning), F-11.4.6 (Wind), F-3.6.74 depends on F-3.5.1 (Procedural Sky)
expectations.text: cross_domain_deps — Features reference dependencies on existing features from other domains
user_interaction_planned — (with_skill only) user-questions.md exists with review questions
